---
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    css: ["default", "extra.css"]
    lib_dir: libs
    seal: false
    nature:
      highlightStyle: github
      highlightLines: true
      countIncrementalSlides: false
      ratio: '16:9'
---

```{r, echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}
##xaringan::inf_mr()
## For offline work: https://bookdown.org/yihui/rmarkdown/some-tips.html#working-offline
## Images not appearing? Put images folder inside the libs folder as that is the main data directory

library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(stargazer)
##library(kableExtra)
##library(modelr)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE,
                      eval = TRUE,
                      error = FALSE,
                      message = FALSE,
                      warning = FALSE,
                      comment = NA)
```

class: slideblue

.size80[**Today's Agenda**]

<br>

.size60[
Review outlines and get ready to write the paper.
]

<br>

.center[.size40[
  Justin Leinaweaver (Spring 2022)
]]



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blue_cubes_lighter2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle, inverse

.pull-left[

<br>
<br>
<br>

.size70[.center[**For Today**]]

<br>
<br>

.size70[.center[**Draft an Outline**]]

]

.pull-right[

.size35[**Intro**]
.size30[
+ What is the question?
+ Why do we care?
+ Thesis statement
]

.size35[**Reason 1 (premise)**]
.size30[
+ Evidence x 2
]

.size35[**Reason 2 (premise)**]
.size30[
+ Evidence x 2
]

.size35[**Reason 3 (premise)**]
.size30[
+ Evidence x 2
]]

???

### Everybody ready to go with this?

#### - Outline in hand?

<br>

Ok, time to get some feedback!

Today you will get feedback from two people and have a chance to revise your outline to reflect that feedback.

In a moment I'll split you into pairs...




---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blue_cubes_lighter2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size55[.center[**Review the Outlines in Pairs**]]

<br>

### Person 1: Present your Outline
.size40[
+ Explain each premise and why you believe the evidence is high quality enough for the purpose
]

???

#### Person 1 presents their paper outline

--

### Person 2: To be convincing to you...
.size40[
+ Are the premises logically tied to the conclusion?
+ Is each premise clear enough?
+ Is each piece of evidence high enough quality?
+ Help them brainstorm a counter-argument
]

???

#### Person 2 gives feedback focused on helping person 1 make it more convincing

<br>

Let's talk about that counter-argument piece (which is new!)

Per the rubric, a convincing argument paper MUST include critical analysis!

One easy way to do that is to add a paragraph focused on rebutting a counter-argument.

- e.g. Here's why my argument might be wrong! And here's how I've addressed those concerns!

<br>

HOWEVER, a good counter-argument should never be a straw man!!

- A counter-argument that ADDS to your paper should begin with the strongest possible version of that argument.

### Make sense?

<br>

*YOU:* Make sure to do this 2x!

- Breakout rooms 16 minutes each, so 7-8 minutes per paper 



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blue_cubes_lighter2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle, inverse

.pull-left[

<br>

<br>

<br>

.size70[.center[**Revise your Outline**]]

]

.pull-right[

.size35[
**Intro**

**Premise 1** (with evidence)

**Premise 2** (with evidence)

**Premise 3** (with evidence)

**Counter-Argument**

**Conclusion**
]]

???

Now that you've heard some more arguments and gotten feedback on yours it's time to revise.

Reflect on the feedback, revise your outline and start writing!

- Tighten up those premises

- Add a counter-argument paragraph

<br>

Paper is due next week!

We'll have a writing workshop in class next Friday so make sure to have a draft done by then.
